name: step-by-step
description: /step-by-step (alias /sbs) — plan-first sequential runbook with THREE modes on a question-density spectrum (fewest → most questions: auto ── manual ── full). AUTO (fewest Q) — show plan, run straight through without pausing. MANUAL (the DEFAULT) — execute ONE step at a time then pause (`⏸ step i/N done · proceed?`) and hand control back; next step runs only on the user's go-ahead. FULL (0.4.0, most Q) — disambiguate FIRST in CHAT FORM (NOT the AskUserQuestion select-box — chat is the default since 0.4.0). Each round = ONE chat question framed with the easy-mode 7-element scaffold (icon · canonical name · friendly alias · plain-line · everyday analogy · ASCII sketch · option compare-table · one-line recommendation) and closed with `→ A · B · 또는 자유응답`. Free-form answers are explicitly OK — the user is never funneled into a fixed grid. Keep asking round-by-round until a scan finds ZERO remaining ambiguity. THEN render a `🎯 합의된 결정셋 (N개)` ASCII tree of every accumulated decision and PAUSE — the user types `go` to confirm or `Qn=<other>` to flip ONLY that decision (re-render after each flip). On `go`, advance to plan execution. Mid-run new ambiguity also opens a chat round + re-renders the agreement screen. (Fallback: a small closed option set on user request may use AskUserQuestion for that one round — chat is the default.) Mode = FIRST arg token `auto` | `manual` | `full` (default manual). `go` is NOT a mode (use `/go` to continue the paused flow). ALL modes halt on a step failure (report failing step + verbatim error + un-run tail) and pause before a genuinely irreversible / destructive / outward-facing step (confirm-then-resume, same bar as the bypass self-check). The deliberate single-threaded counterpart to /cycle's parallel fan-out — one thread, top to bottom.
